title: Use identity precompile (0x4) for cheap memory copy in ERC721 receiver check
trigger_patterns: copy bytes memory to calldata buffer
explanation: The identity precompile at address 0x4 copies input bytes to output — it's a built-in memcpy. staticcall(gas(), 4, src, len, dst, len) copies `len` bytes from `src` to `dst`. This avoids writing a manual copy loop in assembly or paying Solidity's abi.encode overhead. The revert bubble-up pattern uses returndatacopy to forward the inner revert reason to the caller.
when_to_apply: Any assembly block that needs to copy a bytes memory variable to another memory location
when_not_to_apply: Small fixed-size copies where a few mstore calls are cheaper than a precompile call overhead
